data.name: ApplicationAreaRedundancyTitle
data.value: Redundant ApplicationArea on page

data.name: ApplicationAreaRedundancyMessageFormat
data.value: ApplicationArea matches the page-level ApplicationArea and can be removed

data.name: ApplicationAreaRedundancyDescription
data.value: When a ApplicationArea is defined on a page, it automatically applies to all fields of that page. Setting the same ApplicationArea again on individual fields is redundant and adds unnecessary noise to the object definition.

data.name: ApplicationAreaRedundancyCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Remove redundant ApplicationArea

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityMetricTitle
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity metric

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityMetricMessageFormat
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity: {0} (threshold ≥ {1})

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityMetricDescription
data.value: Reports the cyclomatic complexity of a method or trigger. Cyclomatic complexity represents the number of independent execution paths through the code and provides insight into control-flow complexity.

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityThresholdExceededTitle
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity exceeds configured threshold

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityThresholdExceededMessageFormat
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity: {0} (threshold ≥ {1})

data.name: CyclomaticComplexityThresholdExceededDescription
data.value: Cyclomatic complexity measures the number of independent execution paths through a method or trigger. When this value meets or exceeds the configured threshold, the code is considered overly complex, which can reduce readability, increase maintenance cost, and raise the risk of defects.

data.name: DataClassificationRedundancyTitle
data.value: Redundant DataClassification on field

data.name: DataClassificationRedundancyMessageFormat
data.value: DataClassification matches the table-level DataClassification and can be removed.

data.name: DataClassificationRedundancyDescription
data.value: When a DataClassification is defined on a table, it automatically applies to all fields of that table. Setting the same DataClassification again on individual fields is redundant and adds unnecessary noise to the object definition.

data.name: ExplicitlySetRunTriggerTitle
data.value: Explicitly set the RunTrigger parameter on build-in methods

data.name: ExplicitlySetRunTriggerMessageFormat
data.value: The method call '{0}' does not explicitly specify the RunTrigger parameter. Set RunTrigger explicitly instead of the implicit default value of false.

data.name: ExplicitlySetRunTriggerDescription
data.value: This rule enforces explicitly specifying the RunTrigger parameter when calling methods such as Insert, Modify, ModifyAll, Delete, and DeleteAll. These methods allow omitting the RunTrigger parameter, which implicitly defaults to false.

data.name: IdentifiersInEventSubscribersTitle
data.value: Use identifier syntax in event subscriber arguments

data.name: IdentifiersInEventSubscribersMessageFormat
data.value: Event subscriber arguments should use identifier syntax instead of string literals.

data.name: IdentifiersInEventSubscribersDescription
data.value: Starting with Business Central 2023 wave 1 (BC22), event subscriber arguments support identifier syntax rather than string literals. Using identifiers enables full Visual Studio Code navigation features such as Go To Definition and Find All References, improving code readability, refactor safety, and maintainability.

data.name: DataClassificationRedundancyCodeAction
data.value: ALCops: Remove redundant DataClassification

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexMetricTitle
data.value: Maintainability index metric

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexMetricMessageFormat
data.value: Maintainability index: {0} (threshold ≤ {1})

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexMetricDescription
data.value: Reports the maintainability index of a method or trigger. The maintainability index is a composite metric derived from cyclomatic complexity, Halstead volume, and code size, providing an overall indication of how easy the code is to understand, modify, and maintain.

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexThresholdExceededTitle
data.value: Maintainability index below configured threshold

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexThresholdExceededMessageFormat
data.value: Maintainability index: {0} (threshold ≤ {1})

data.name: MaintainabilityIndexThresholdExceededDescription
data.value: The maintainability index provides an overall indication of code maintainability based on complexity, size, and structural characteristics. When this value falls at or below the configured threshold, the code is considered difficult to maintain, increasing the likelihood of defects and long-term maintenance cost.

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationTitle
data.value: Do not use Object IDs as object references

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationMessageFormat
data.value: Object ID '{0}' used as object reference. Use the object name '{1}' instead.

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationFormatWithoutCodeFix
data.value: Object ID '{0}' used as object reference.

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationDescription
data.value: Using Object IDs in variable or property declarations reduces readability and prevents safe renaming. Object IDs are numeric and provide no semantic meaning, making the code harder to understand and maintain. Declare variables and properties using the resolved object name rather than a numeric Object ID.

data.name: ObjectIdInDeclarationActionTitle
data.value: ALCops: Replace id with name of object reference

data.name: RecordInstanceIsolationLevelTitle
data.value: Use ReadIsolation instead of LockTable

data.name: RecordInstanceIsolationLevelMessageFormat
data.value: Use ReadIsolation(IsolationLevel::UpdLock) instead of LockTable().

data.name: RecordInstanceIsolationLevelDescription
data.value: Use the ReadIsolation method to control isolation level on database transactions instead of the LockTable method.

data.name: RecordInstanceIsolationLevelCodeActionTitle
data.value: ALCops: Replace LockTable() with ReadIsolation

data.name: UseSecretTextForSensitiveTextTitle
data.value: Use SecretText type to protect credentials and sensitive textual values from being revealed

data.name: UseSecretTextForSensitiveTextMessageFormat
data.value: Sensitive textual values should be passed as SecretText to prevent them from being revealed.

data.name: UseSecretTextForSensitiveTextDescription
data.value: Using SecretText prevents confidential information such as API keys, tokens, passwords, and similar secrets from being exposed through the AL debugger during regular or snapshot debugging.
